summary: Unit tests cover core requirements (glob patterns, labels, priority, deduplication, basic errors) with 17 tests and good fixture setup. Missing coverage: config parsing edge cases (whitespace, comments, malformed rules), special characters in patterns/labels/paths, character classes, question-mark wildcards, and meaningful error message verification. Assertions use fragile substring matching ([[ output == *label* ]]) instead of exact output format verification (e.g., test would pass if output format changed to "api, documentation" with spaces). Integration test harness assumes exactly 17 tests (hard-coded in run_act_tests.sh) but test_workflow_structure.bats adds 11 more tests, making harness expectations brittle. Workflow structure tests are superficial (grep-based). Would catch basic regressions but miss output formatting issues, config parsing bugs, and edge cases with whitespace or special characters.
